project.title: Protein-protein interactions of p21 in MCF7 cells during cellular senescence
project.projectDescription: A mass spectrometry-based proteomics analysis was performed to study the protein binders of p21 in MCF7 cells during the progression and maintenance of cellular senescence.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Anti-p21 pull-downs were performed in replicating MCF7 cells or cells treated with benzo[a]pyrene for 48 h or 120 h. Corresponding pull-downs using isotype control antibodies were also performed. Proteins were digested in-gel by trypsin. The resultant peptides were then dimethyl labelled and combined. Peptides were measured on an Exploris 480 Orbitrap mass spectrometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw data files were processed by MaxQuant software package (version 1.6.17.0) using Andromeda search engine. Spectral data were searched against a target-decoy database consisting of the forward and reverse sequences of UniProt H. sapien reference proteome release 2020_01 (96,833 entries), and a list of 246 common contaminants. Corresponding labels were selected for “Light” (DimethLys0 and DimethNter0) and “Medium” (DimethLys4 and DimethNter4) labels. False discovery rate (FDR) was set to 1% at both peptide and protein levels. The “re-quantify” function was switched on.
